# Transcrição: 3_powerful_rules_of_communication

- **Fonte:** 3_powerful_rules_of_communication
- **Duração:** 01:22
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:22]

Let's talk about these things one at a time. The first thing is you have to believe in what you're saying. This is the golden rule when it comes to speaking. Only talk about things you actually care about. And a lot of the times when you see people speak on stage, generally it's not what they believe in because it's not what they want to talk about. It's something their manager has given them to talk about. It's something their boss has given them, an agenda that the CEO has given them to speak about. Therefore, it doesn't seem like they're speaking from the gut because they actually don't believe in the things they're saying. The second part is that you have to connect with your message. Now, this is a question of rehearsal. A lot of the times when people are speaking, they are so worried about what they're saying next to the point where that's all they're focused on. You're not focused on feeling your words. You're not focused on connecting with your words. All you're focused on is, holy crap, what's coming next? When you do this, then all of a sudden now, what you're saying, that's easy. You know what's coming next. You know it like the back of your hand. And when you know it like the back of your hand, all of a sudden now, you can use all that cognitive energy that you have to focus on feeling the words to make sure that you're connected with the message you're about to deliver. And the third thing, connecting with your audience. What this essentially means is, it has to be about the audience. If you go on stage and you just keep talking about you, me, me, me, me, me, me, me, me, keep talking about your achievements, your histories, your stories, and you don't make it about the audience, then there's no connection.
